ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the marital status of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "income" INTEGER /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "children" INTEGER /* Number of children in the customer's household */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the highest level of education completed by the customer */,
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the occupation of the customer */,
  "home_owner" INTEGER /* Whether the customer owns a home */,
  "cars" INTEGER /* Number of cars owned by the customer */,
  "commute_distance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the commute distance of the customer */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region where the customer resides */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "age_bracket_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the age bracket of the customer */,
  "purchased_bike" INTEGER /* Whether the customer has purchased a bike */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "signup_date" TEXT /* Date when the customer signed up */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login by the customer */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points accumulated by the customer */,
  "preferred_contact_method" TEXT /* Preferred method of contact for the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "customer_segment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_age_bracket_id FOREIGN KEY ("age_bracket_id") REFERENCES age_brackets ("age_bracket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_commute_distance_id FOREIGN KEY ("commute_distance_id") REFERENCES commute_distances ("commute_distance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_marital_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("marital_status_id") REFERENCES marital_status ("marital_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_occupation_id FOREIGN KEY ("occupation_id") REFERENCES occupations ("occupation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_customer_segment_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_segment_id") REFERENCES customer_segments ("customer_segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marital_status" (
  "marital_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marital status */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer (e.g., Married, Single) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("marital_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed by the customer (e.g., Bachelors, Partial College) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "occupations" (
  "occupation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each occupation */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer (e.g., Skilled Manual, Professional) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("occupation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "commute_distances" (
  "commute_distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each commute distance */,
  "commute_distance" TEXT /* Commute distance of the customer (e.g., 0-1 Miles, 5-10 Miles) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("commute_distance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Region where the customer resides (e.g., Europe, Pacific) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "age_brackets" (
  "age_bracket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age bracket */,
  "age_bracket" TEXT /* Age bracket of the customer (e.g., Middle Age, Old) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_bracket_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., Email, Phone Call) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel of the interaction (e.g., Email, Social Media) */,
  "interaction_details" TEXT /* Details of the interaction */,
  "response_status" INTEGER /* Whether the interaction received a response */,
  "interaction_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction (e.g., Sale, Follow-up Needed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the purchase */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the purchase */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product purchased */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product purchased */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount of the purchase */,
  "discount_applied" REAL /* Discount applied to the purchase */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used for the purchase */,
  "return_status" INTEGER /* Whether the product was returned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "stock_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product in stock */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "customer_segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer segment */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Description of the customer segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the user role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access */,
  "action_performed" TEXT /* Description of the action performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_log_id")
)
